entries.title: True Sith Empire Survives Beyond KOTOR

content.fact: KOTOR-era hints point toward a surviving Sith power beyond the known Republic-Sith war.
content.injection: Before the Sith Empire returns openly, treat the True Sith as hidden, fragmentary knowledge rather than common galactic fact.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Dark Wars.
Respect story-position bounds from Dark Wars Begin to Great Galactic War Begins.
antiLore: Do not make SWTOR’s Sith Empire common public knowledge during KOTOR unless the story has revealed it.
Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.

entries.title: Great Galactic War Returns Sith Empire Openly

content.fact: The reconstituted Sith Empire returns as an open galactic superpower and forces the Republic into a large-scale war.
content.injection: During the Great Galactic War, treat the Sith Empire as a state actor with fleets, armies, intelligence services, academies, and territory.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Great Galactic War.
Respect story-position bounds from Great Galactic War Begins to Sacking of Coruscant.
antiLore: Do not skip ahead to later character, faction, relationship, location, or event states.

entries.title: Sacking Coruscant Shocks Republic Into Treaty

content.fact: The Sacking of Coruscant devastates the Republic capital and forces the Treaty of Coruscant under humiliating conditions.
content.injection: At the Sacking of Coruscant, write the Republic and Jedi as shocked, damaged, and politically forced into compromise.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Great Galactic War.
Respect story-position bounds from Sacking of Coruscant to Sacking of Coruscant.
antiLore: Do not skip ahead to later character, faction, relationship, location, or event states.

entries.title: Treaty Creates Cold War, Not Peace

content.fact: The Treaty of Coruscant produces a tense cold war full of proxy conflicts, espionage, and border crises rather than real peace.
content.injection: During SWTOR cold-war context, keep overt galactic war constrained but make covert operations, local wars, and political sabotage common.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Old Republic Cold War.
Respect story-position bounds from Treaty of Coruscant and Cold War to SWTOR Class-Story Era.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.

entries.title: Jedi Relocate to Tython After Coruscant

content.fact: After Coruscant’s devastation, the Jedi Order re-centers training and reflection on Tython in SWTOR-era context.
content.injection: During SWTOR cold-war scenes, Tython can function as the Jedi spiritual and training center while Coruscant remains politically central but wounded.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Old Republic Cold War.
Respect story-position bounds from Treaty of Coruscant and Cold War to SWTOR Class-Story Era.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.

entries.title: Dromund Kaas Is Sith Imperial Capital

content.fact: Dromund Kaas is the Sith Empire’s storm-wrapped capital and a center of Imperial military, political, and dark-side authority.
content.injection: In SWTOR-era scenes, use Dromund Kaas as the seat of Sith Imperial power, intrigue, hierarchy, and dark-side atmosphere.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: SWTOR Era.
Respect story-position bounds from Great Galactic War Begins to Eternal Empire Crisis.
antiLore: Do not skip ahead to later character, faction, relationship, location, or event states.

content.fact: Vitiate/Valkorion operates as more than a normal Sith Emperor; his deeper goals and identities are high-risk spoiler material.
content.injection: Before the relevant SWTOR context reveals it, do not casually expose Vitiate’s full nature, Valkorion identity, or galaxy-consuming ambitions.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: SWTOR Era.
Respect story-position bounds from Great Galactic War Begins to Eternal Empire Crisis.
antiLore: Do not spoil Valkorion/Eternal Empire material in early SWTOR cold-war context.
Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.

entries.title: Malgus Embodies Imperial Reform Through War

content.fact: Darth Malgus represents a militarized, reformist Sith threat willing to break Imperial norms when they limit conquest.
content.injection: When Malgus is relevant, write him as direct, martial, visionary, and dangerous to both Republic enemies and stagnant Sith institutions.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: SWTOR Era.
Respect story-position bounds from Great Galactic War Begins to SWTOR Class-Story Era.
antiLore: Do not solve the scene by ignoring this rule unless user-authored continuity overrides it.

content.fact: Satele Shan carries Shan/Revan-era legacy while serving as a central Jedi leader during the Sith Empire crisis.
content.injection: In SWTOR-era scenes, write Satele as a senior Jedi balancing inherited legacy, wartime burden, and the need to rebuild Jedi confidence.

entries.title: SWTOR Class Era Is Multi-Protagonist State

content.fact: SWTOR-era story state can vary by class path, allegiance, and player route; a deck should avoid assuming one universal protagonist unless a route is selected.
content.injection: When using SWTOR material, preserve route ambiguity: avoid declaring one class-story protagonist as present or dominant unless the chat specifies that route.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: SWTOR Era.
Respect story-position bounds from SWTOR Class-Story Era to Revanite Crisis.
antiLore: Do not solve the scene by ignoring this rule unless user-authored continuity overrides it.

entries.title: Revanite Crisis Reintroduces Revan as Symbol

content.fact: The Revanite crisis turns Revan into a cross-faction symbol and destabilizing obsession rather than only a KOTOR-era figure.
content.injection: During Revanite crisis context, write Revan’s legacy as contested by cultists, Republic actors, Sith, and survivors of older wars.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Revanite Crisis.
Respect story-position bounds from Revanite Crisis to Revanite Crisis.
antiLore: Do not skip ahead to later character, faction, relationship, location, or event states.

content.fact: The Eternal Empire crisis introduces a third imperial power that can dominate both Republic and Sith Empire assumptions.
content.injection: In Eternal Empire context, do not reduce the conflict to Republic versus Sith; Zakuul and the Eternal Fleet can impose a new galactic order.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Eternal Empire Crisis.
Respect story-position bounds from Eternal Empire Crisis to Eternal Empire Crisis.

entries.title: Zakuul Is Not Normal Sith World

content.fact: Zakuul is a distinct Eternal Empire society, not simply another Sith academy world or Republic planet.
content.injection: When Zakuul is active, portray it with its own mythology, civic order, Knights, technology, and relationship to Valkorion.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Eternal Empire Crisis.
Respect story-position bounds from Eternal Empire Crisis to Eternal Empire Crisis.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.

entries.title: Late New Sith Wars Point Toward Bane, Not Films

content.fact: The late New Sith Wars and Ruusan transition set up Darth Bane’s Rule of Two, but remain far before film-era Sith politics.
content.injection: In late Old Republic context, allow the Sith to collapse toward Bane’s reform, but do not import Palpatine, Clone Wars, or Galactic Empire state.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Late New Sith Wars.
Respect story-position bounds from Late New Sith Wars to Ruusan Reformation and Old Republic End-State.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.
